title: auth — trait composition
description: Two owned traits. `HasScopeSuffixResolution` is a marker on services that resolve `.own` / `.tenant` / `.branch` / `.team` / `.child` / `.any` permission suffixes into query filters. `HasSessionRevocation` is a composable helper for models whose state change must revoke every active PAT for the referring User (e.g. User suspension, role revocation, permission changes with security implications).

owned.description: Marker trait composed by every service that consults `ScopeSuffixResolver` at query-build time. Adds no accessor / mutator / scope of its own — signals reflection-based tooling (integration tests, static analysis) that the class MUST NOT bypass the resolver. Every base repository in `framework/crud` implicitly composes this via its abstract parent.

Composable helper for models that must revoke every active PAT for the referring User(s) when their state mutates. Ships: `->revokeAllSessions(SessionRevocationReason $reason)`, `->revokeSessionsForApplication(string $applicationId, SessionRevocationReason $reason)`. Each method is idempotent, transactional, and fires `AllSessionsRevoked` (bulk) OR `SessionRevoked` (single) after commit. Typical consumers: `user::User` (on suspended → revoke sessions), `access::Role` (on role deleted — revoke sessions for holders), `access::Permission` (rare — on manual permission removal).
usedBy: auth::internal use for cross-Application PasswordChanged fan-out
owned.wraps: Wraps Sanctum's `->tokens()->delete()` in an idempotent domain operation that fires SessionRevoked events per token so downstream listeners (activity, notifications, observability) all see the revocation.

framework.reason: Refresh tokens, password resets, verifications keep a bounded soft-delete window for audit trails; hard-delete is scheduled per retention.json.

notes: NO BelongsToTenant — refresh tokens reference User / PlatformUser polymorphically; tenant / application scope flows through the tokenable.
The soft-delete substrate keeps the row 30 days after consumption/revocation for audit; hard-deleted by PurgeExpiredRefreshTokensJob.
NO SoftDeletes — password reset tokens are HARD-deleted 24h after consume/expiry (no audit value).
Same shape as password resets — hard-deleted 30 days after consume/expiry.
NO SoftDeletes — challenges are ephemeral (5-min TTL); hard-deleted 1h after consume/expiry.
HasMetadata carries the pending-login context (application_id, tenant_id, requested_scope) so the login flow can resume after verification.
300-second TTL. Hard-deleted 24h after consume/expiry. HasActivityLog because grant issuance + exchange are audit-critical (they bridge Applications).
NO BelongsToTenant — references identity_id + source/target application_ids directly.
Deny-list entries live until the underlying JWT's natural `exp` passes — no need to keep them longer (verifiers reject expired JWTs on their own).
HasActivityLog because manual denies are compliance-audited.
Retired signing keys kept 180 days after `retired_at` (still needed for verification during rotation windows).
`secret_encrypted` is KMS-envelope-encrypted at rest — the plaintext HS256 secret NEVER touches Postgres.
